input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android API 35+), edge-to-edge display is enforced, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The new approach uses `WindowInsets` and the `Ime` inset type to handle keyboard visibility.


In .NET 10, the solution requires listening to window insets changes and adjusting padding dynamically when the keyboard appears, since the old resize mode no longer works on Android 15+. I'll need to show how to implement `ViewCompat.SetOnApplyWindowInsetsListener` to handle IME insets properly.
